mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings pressure, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicates strong consumer spending supported by accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains flat, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during early-cycle recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the predictive power reverses to negative direction in the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 230.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for nominal GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized demand and supply expansion, reducing inventory bottlenecks and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and INDPRO uptrend fails to predict positive returns when housing starts rise but industrial production stagnates, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product confirms economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during late-cycle downturns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle downturns, or the signal produces positive returns in more than 40% of contraction quarters.
